channel.title: JLT What's Trending — LinkedIn
channel.description: What's Trending in Travel — LinkedIn Business edition

item.title: UK travel in summer 2026 has a clear shape, and it's worth agents understanding what's driving it.
item.description:
UK travel in summer 2026 has a clear shape, and it's worth agents understanding what's driving it.
Domestic searches are up over 11% year on year. The average booked international trip sits around 317 miles from home. Cost of living is the primary planning factor for 42% of UK adults.
Three actions agents can take from this week's trending data:
- Shoulder season is the saving that costs clients nothing but flexibility. Push Autumn 2026 departures now, with a clear GBP£ comparison - Lead the property conversation. 27% of travellers now choose accommodation that is part of the experience itself, not a backdrop to it - Ask the multi-gen question on every family enquiry. 31% are travelling with extended family this year, and the right property for that group takes some finding
The full breakdown is in this week's What's Trending blog.
Blog link (first comment, once live): https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/whats-trending-in-travel-short-haul-shoulder-season-2026-07-02

item.title: UK travellers are not pulling back on summer 2026. They are making sharper choices about when, where and how they spend.
item.description:
UK travellers are not pulling back on summer 2026. They are making sharper choices about when, where and how they spend.
Three things our agents are working with this week:
- Date-shifting is real. Thirty-one percent of UK adults planning summer travel are choosing off-peak dates. Late August and early September represent strong value windows to present to families.
- Mountain and lake escapes are growing fast. Seventy-one percent of travellers are considering this category for summer or autumn. A cooler, calmer alternative to busy beach resorts and a genuine upsell for the right client.
- Multi-generational travel is now a distinct booking pattern. Villas, connecting suites, clustered cruise cabins are three formats worth having ready for families travelling across households.
The full breakdown of this week's trends is on the blog.
Blog link (post in your first comment, once live):
https://janinelovestravel.co.uk/whats-trending-in-travel-smart-value-summer-2026-06-25

item.title: UK travellers are not booking less in 2026. They are booking more carefully.
item.description:
UK travellers are not booking less in 2026. They are booking more carefully.
Four trends our agents are drawing on this week:
- 42% of travellers are booking earlier to lock in value; 40% are choosing off-peak timing (Criteo 2026) - Scandinavia and cooler-climate destinations are growing fast… Scandinavia bookings tipped to rise up to 35% this year - The "one big trip" pattern is gaining ground… 12 to 14 night escapes, twin-centres, and long-haul bucket-list itineraries are the shape clients are asking for - Travellers browse 25 hotel listings on average before deciding… a structured three-option quote is the most effective response
Helping clients be smart with their timing and their money is where great agent relationships are built. The full trend breakdown is on the JLT blog this week.
[Blog link to be added in comments after 24-48 hrs]
